name: Da Ono Hawaiian Food
tagline: The original Ono Hawaiian Foods closed after 57 years, but Da Ono carries the torch at the same Kapahulu address — kalua pig still hits.
mustOrder: Kalua pig plate — smoky, tender, and authentic as it gets
Lomi salmon — fresh tomato and salmon, served cold the traditional way
Pipikaula short ribs — Hawaiian-style dried beef, salty and chewy
parking: Street parking on Kapahulu Ave, small lot behind building
talkStory: The original Ono Hawaiian Foods was founded by Sueko Ono in 1962 and ran for 57 years, becoming one of Obama's favorite Honolulu stops (he'd order the kalua pig and lomi salmon). After it closed in 2019, Da Ono opened at the same Kapahulu address, carrying the torch of traditional Hawaiian plate lunches. Wed-Sun only, and popular items sell out by 2pm. Order the combo plate to try multiple dishes — the poi is hand-pounded and the pipikaula is legit.
